questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main
questions.q: A car is negotiating a curved road of radius <math> <mrow> <mtext>R</mtext></mrow> </math>. The road is banked at an angle <math><mi mathvariant="normal">θ</mi></math>. The coefficient of friction between the tyres of the car and the road is <math><msub><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">μ</mi></mrow><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">s</mi></mrow></msub></math>. The maximum safe velocity on this road is:
questions.solution:
N=mgcos⁡θ+mv2Rsin⁡θ
f max =μN ⇒ fmax= μ s mgcos⁡θ+ μ s mv2Rsin⁡θ
mg⁡sinθ+fmax=mv2R⁡cosθ
mgsin⁡θ+ μ s mgcos⁡θ+ μ s mv2Rsin⁡θ=mv2Rcos⁡θ
gsin⁡θ+ μ s gcos⁡θ=v2Rcos⁡θ- μ s sin⁡θ
gRtan⁡θ+ μ s 1- μ s tan⁡θ=v2
questions.source: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main
questions.q: Two blocks A and B of masses 3m and m respectively are connected by a massless and inextensible string. The whole system is suspended by a massless spring as shown in figure. The magnitudes of acceleration of A and B immediately after the string is cut, are respectively
questions.solution:
Before the string is cut, tension is acting in the string in downward position.
kx=T+3mg ......(i)
T=mg ......(ii)

⇒     kx=4mg
After the string is cut, T=0

a=kx-3mg3m
a=4mg-3mg3m
Acceleration of Ais a=g3↑ and acceleration of B is a=mgm=g↓.
questions.source: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main

The coefficients of static and kinetic friction between the box and the plank will be, respectively:

μs=tan⁡30o=13
⇒μs=0.57=0.6
S=ut+12at2
4=12a42⇒a=12=0.5
a=g sinθ-μkgcosθ
⇒μK=0.93≈0.5

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main
questions.q: A roller coaster is designed such that riders experience "weightlessness" as they go round the top of a hill whose radius of curvature is $20 \mathrm{~m}$. The speed of the car at the top of the hill is between
questions.solution:
Balancing the forces, we get
$$ M g-N=M \frac{v^{2}}{R} $$ For weightlessness, $N=0$ $\therefore$ $$ \frac{M v^{2}}{R}=M g $$ where $R$ is the radius of curvature and $v$ is the speed of car. Therefore, $$ v=\sqrt{\mathrm{Rg}} $$ Putting the values, $R=20 \mathrm{~m}, g=10.0 \mathrm{~m} / \mathrm{s}^{2}$ So, $\quad v=\sqrt{20 \times 10.0}=14.14 \mathrm{~m} / \mathrm{s}^{2}$ Thus, the speed of the car at the top of the hill is $$ \text { between } 14 \mathrm{~m} / \mathrm{s} \text { and } 15 \mathrm{~m} / \mathrm{s} \text { . } $$ Note : The roller coaster is a popular amusement ride developed for amusement parks and modern theme parks.
questions.source: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main
questions.q: A block of mass $10 \mathrm{~kg}$ is placed on a horizontal frictionless surface and is attached to a cord which passes over two light frictionless pulleys as shown in the figure. The hanging block tied to the other end of the cord is initially at rest $2 \mathrm{~m}$ above the horizontal floor.If the hanging block strikes the floor $2 \mathrm{~s}$ after the system is released, then weight of the hanging block is ....... $\left(g=10 \mathrm{~ms}^{-2}\right)$
questions.source: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main
questions.q: The masses of blocks $\mathrm{A}$ and $\mathrm{B}$ are $\mathrm{m}$ and $\mathrm{M}$ respectively. Between $\mathrm{A}$ and $\mathrm{B}$, there is a constant frictional force $\mathrm{F}$ and $\mathrm{B}$ can slide on a smooth horizontal surface. A is set in motion with velocity while $\mathrm{B}$ is at rest. What is the distance moved by A relative to B before they move with the same velocity?
questions.solution:
For the blocks A and B FBD as shown below

Equations of motion
$\begin{array}{l}
\mathrm{a}_{\mathrm{A}}=\frac{\mathrm{F}}{\mathrm{M}}(\text { in }-\mathrm{x} \text { direction }) \\
\mathrm{a}_{\mathrm{B}}=\frac{\mathrm{F}}{\mathrm{M}}(\text { in }+\mathrm{x} \text { direction) }
\end{array}$
Relative acceleration, of A w.r.t. B,
$\begin{aligned}
\mathrm{a}_{\mathrm{A}, \mathrm{B}} &=\mathrm{a}_{\mathrm{A}}-\mathrm{a}_{\mathrm{B}}=-\frac{\mathrm{F}}{\mathrm{m}}-\frac{\mathrm{F}}{\mathrm{M}} \\
&=-\mathrm{F}\left(\frac{\mathrm{M}+\mathrm{m}}{\mathrm{Mm}}\right)(\text { along }-\mathrm{x} \text { direction })
\end{aligned}$
Initial relative velocity of A w.r.t. B, $\mathrm{u}_{\mathrm{AB}}=\mathrm{v}_{0}$
using equation $v^{2}=u^{2}+2$ as
$0=v_{0}^{2}-\frac{2 \mathrm{~F}(\mathrm{~m}+\mathrm{M}) \mathrm{S}}{\mathrm{Mm}} \Rightarrow \mathrm{S}=\frac{\mathrm{Mmv}_{0}^{2}}{2 \mathrm{~F}(\mathrm{~m}+\mathrm{M})}$
i.e., Distance moved by A relative to B
$\mathrm{S}_{\mathrm{AB}}=\frac{\mathrm{Mmv}_{0}^{2}}{2 \mathrm{~F}(\mathrm{~m}+\mathrm{M})}$
questions.source: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main
questions.q: As shown in the figure, two particles, each of mass 'm' tied at the ends of a light string of length 2a are kept on a frictionless horizontal surface. When the mid point (P) of the string is pulled vertically upwards with a small but constant force F, the particles move towards each other on the surface. Magnitude of acceleration of each particle, when the separation between them becomes 2x is
questions.solution:
From the above diagram,

tanθ=a2-x2x, 2Tsinθ=F and Tcosθ=ma

from the above equations,

a=Fcotθ2m

⇒a=F2m1tanθ=F2mxa2-x2
questions.source: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main
questions.q:
The velocity $(v)$ of a particle (under a force $F$ ) depends on its distance $(x)$ from the origin (with $x>0) v \propto\frac{1}{\sqrt{x}}$. Find how the magnitude
of the force $(F)$ on the particle depends on $x ?$
questions.solution:
According to the question.
$$
v \propto \frac{1}{\sqrt{x}}
$$
or $v=\frac{k}{\sqrt{x}}$
$$
\frac{d v}{d t}=\frac{d}{d t} \cdot \frac{K}{\sqrt{x}}=k \cdot \frac{-1}{2} \cdot x^{-\frac{1}{2}-1} \cdot \frac{d x}{d t}
$$
$a=-\frac{k}{2} \cdot x^{-\frac{3}{2}} \cdot \frac{k}{\sqrt{x}}\left[\because \frac{d x}{d t}=v\right.$ and $v=\frac{k}{\sqrt{x}}$ from $\left.\mathrm{Eq}(i)\right]$
$$
=\frac{-k^{2}}{2} \cdot \frac{1}{x^{2}}
$$
$=m \frac{k^{2}}{2} \cdot \frac{1}{x^{2}}$
(magnitue
$F=\frac{k^{2}}{2} \cdot \frac{m}{x^{2}}$
$\therefore$
$F x \frac{1}{x^{2}}$
questions.source: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main

questions.q: A block mass $m$ is placed on a smooth wedge of inclination $\theta$. The whole system is accelerated horizontally so that the block does not slip on the wedge. The force exerted by the wedge on the block ( $g$ is acceleration due to gravity) will be:
questions.solution:
Here $N \cos \theta=m g$
$\Rightarrow N=\frac{m g}{\cos \theta}$

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main
questions.q: Same spring is attached with $2 \mathrm{~kg}, 3 \mathrm{~kg}$ and $1 \mathrm{~kg}$ blocks in three different cases as shown in figure. If $x_1, x_2, x_3$ be the extensions in the spring in the three cases, then
questions.solution: If $T_1, T_2, T_3$ are the tensions in the strings in the three cases, we have $\begin{aligned} & T_1=\frac{2 m_1 m_2 g}{m_1+m_2}=\frac{2 \times 2 \times 2 g}{(2+2)}=2 g \\ & T_2=\frac{2 \times 3 \times 2 g}{(3+2)}=2.4 g \\ & T_3=\frac{2 \times 1 \times 2 g}{(1+2)}=1.33 g \\ & \text { As } x \propto T \text { and } T_2>T_1>T_3 \\ & \therefore \quad x_2>x_1>x_3\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main
questions.q: Which of the following is the correct order of forces?
options: Weak force <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mo><</mo></math> gravitational forces <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mo><</mo></math> strong force (nuclear) <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mo><</mo></math> electrostatic force

Gravitational force <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mo><</mo></math> weak force <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mo><</mo></math>(electrostatic)<math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mo><</mo></math> strong force

Gravitational force <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mo><</mo></math> electrostatic force<math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mo><</mo></math> weak force <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mo><</mo></math> strong force

Weak force <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mo><</mo></math> gravitational force <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mo><</mo></math> electrostatic force<math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mo><</mo></math> strong force
questions.solution:
Strong nuclear force, electrostatic force, weak force and gravitational force are the four fundamental forces. Among these fundamental forces, strong nuclear forces which holds the two positive protons inside the nucleus are the strongest followed by electrostatic force. Also, gravitational force is weakest among all.

Hence, correct order of force is Gravitational force<Weak Nuclear Force<Electrostatic force<Strong nuclear force.
questions.source: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main
questions.q: A block of mass <math><mn>10</mn><mi mathvariant="normal"> </mi><mi mathvariant="normal">k</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">g</mi></math> is in contact against the inner wall of a hollow cylindrical drum of radius<math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mo> </mo><mn>1</mn><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">m</mi></math>. The coefficient of friction between the block and the inner wall of the cylinder is <math><mn>0.1</mn></math>. The minimum angular velocity needed for the cylinder to keep the block stationary when the cylinder is vertical and rotating about its axis will be  <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfenced separators="|"><mrow><mi>g</mi><mo>=</mo><mn>10</mn><mtext>  </mtext><mi mathvariant="normal">m</mi><mo> </mo><msup><mi mathvariant="normal">s</mi><mrow><mo>-</mo><mn>2</mn></mrow></msup></mrow></mfenced></math>,
questions.solution:
​​​​​​​
N=mω2R
f=mg
For minimum angular velocity, 
f=μN
mg=μ·mω2R
ω=gμR=100.1×1=10 rad s-1
questions.source: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main
questions.q: A body of mass 200 gram is tied to a spring of spring constant $12.5 \mathrm{~N} / \mathrm{m}$, while other end of spring is fixed at point ' $\mathrm{O}$ '. If the body moves about ' $\mathrm{O}$ ' in a circular path on a smooth horizontal surface with constant angular speed $5 \mathrm{rad} / \mathrm{s}$ then the ratio of extension in the spring to its natural length will be
questions.solution:
Let the normal length be $\mathrm{L}$ and the extension be $\mathrm{x}$.
$\therefore \quad$ Restoring Force $=$ Centripetal Force
$\begin{aligned}
& \mathrm{kx}=\mathrm{m}(\mathrm{L}+\mathrm{x}) \omega^2 \\
& 12.5 \mathrm{x}=0.2(\mathrm{~L}+\mathrm{x}) 25 \quad \ldots(\because \omega=5 \mathrm{rad} / \mathrm{s})
\end{aligned}$
$\begin{aligned} & 12.5 x=5(L+x) \\ & 7.5 x=5 L \\ \therefore \quad & \frac{x}{L}=\frac{5}{7.5}=\frac{2}{3}=2: 3\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main
questions.q: Three blocks are placed at rest on a smooth inclined plane with force acting on m1 parallel to the inclined plane. Find the contact force between m2 and m3
questions.solution:
F = m 1 + m 2 + m 3 g sin  θ                .... (1)

N = m 3 g sin  θ                .... (2)

Dividing Eq. (2) by Eq. (1), we get

N = m 3 F m 1 + m 2 + m 3

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main
questions.q: In the figure, the mass of a ball is 95 times the mass of the rod. Length of the rod is 1 m. The level of ball is the same as rod level. Find out the time taken by the ball to reach at the upper end of the rod.
questions.solution:
Let a1  and  a2 be accelerations of a ball (upward) and rod (downward), respectively.



Clearly, from the diagram

2a1-a2 ...(i)

Now, for the ball

2T-95mg-95ma ...(ii)

and for the rod, mg-T=ma2 ...(iii)

On solving equations (i) and (iii), we get

a1=g29 m s-2↑ (upward)

a2=2g29 m s-2↓ (downward)

So, acceleration of ball w.r.t rod =a1+a2=3g29

Now, displacement of ball w.r.t. rod when it reaches the upper end of rod is 1m .

Using the equation of motion,

s=ut+12at2

s=0+12×3×1029t2

t=5830=1.4 s approx
questions.source: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main
questions.q: The friction coefficient between the board and the floor shown in diagram is μ. Find the maximum force that the man can exert on the rope, so that the board does not slip on the floor.
questions.solution:
The forces acting on the system are shown in the diagram

For vertical equilibrium of the point P

T = F                      ....(i)



And for the vertical equilibrium of the system



R + T = (m+M)g,  i.e.,   R = (m+M)g - T             ....(ii)

Now the system will not move horizontally till

               T < fL

i.e.,         T < μ[m+Mg - T]               fL = μR

which on simplification gives

T<μm+Mg1+μ

So in the light of equation (i), we get

Fmax=μm+Mg1+μ
questions.source: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main
questions.q: A car moves uniformly along a horizontal sine curve <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>y</mi><mo>=</mo><mi>a</mi><mi>sin</mi><mo>(</mo><mi>x</mi><mo>/</mo><mi>α</mi><mo>)</mo></math>, where <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>a</mi></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>α</mi></math> are certain constants. The coefficient of friction between the wheels and the road is equal to <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>k</mi></math>. The maximum constant speed at which the car can travel without sliding is
questions.solution:
Step I : Determine the radius of curvature of given curve at point P (x, y).

∵        y = a sin x α ∴ d⁡ y d⁡ x = a α cos x α

∴       d⁡ 2 y d⁡ x 2 = - a α 2 sin x α

∴    the radius of curvature is   R⁡ = 1 + d⁡ y d⁡ x 2 3 / 2 d⁡ 2 y d⁡ x 2

                                              R⁡ = 1 + a 2 α 2 cos 2 x a 3 / 2 - a α 2 sin x α         ...(i)

Step II: Relate the friction and centripetal acceleration. In this case, centripetal acceleration is provided by force of friction.

∵                              f ≤ fL     or f ≤ kmg

or                               mv 2 R⁡ ≤ kmg or v⁡ 2 R⁡ ≤ kg

∴                               v⁡ max 2 R⁡ min = k g

∴                               v⁡ max = k g R⁡ min

From equation (i), for the minimum value of the radius of curvature,

                               x α = π 2

∴                               R⁡ min = α 2 a                              (from equation. (i))

∴                               v⁡ max = k g α 2 a = α k g a
questions.source: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main

questions.q:
As shown in the following figures, block of mass 2 kg is in equilibrium in the vertical plane.




At a certain instant right spring in case-1 and right string in case-2 are cut. Then the ratio of instantaneous acceleration of block in case-1 to case-2 just after the cutting is (strings and springs are ideal)
questions.solution:
2Tsin⁡37°=20

2T×35=20

T=503 N

Now when spring is cut in case-1 then net force F1 acting on block is,

F1=202+5032-2×20×503×35                    (resultant force of the remaining T=503 N and mg=20 N forces)

=503 N

Similarly, when the string is cut in case-2 then net force F2 acting on block is F2=20×45=16 N

∴a1a2=503×116=5048=2524
